summary:
Major brain gangliosides like GM1 and GD3 contribute to Alzheimer’s pathology through effects on synapses and amyloid-beta accumulation, indicating plausible mechanisms but no proven therapeutic benefit by early 2019. Despite biologic rationale, there was no approved therapy or advanced-phase trial targeting these molecules at that time. 

The summary is correct because it accurately reflects only the clinically pertinent facts from Agent2's analysis without adding external claims or interpretations beyond what was provided. It directly addresses whether gangliosides were actual treatment targets—as per the requirement—and focuses solely on available data up until February 2019.
